title: asperula cynanchica var. alpina
intro: Asperula cynanchica var. alpina, also known as Alpine Wood Sorrel, is a plant species belonging to the Rubus family and is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. It has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, due to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Alpine Wood Sorrel include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its potential to protect against oxidative stress and promote wound healing. Alpine Wood Sorrel is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in herbal remedies to support digestive health and reduce symptoms of fever and colds.
